title: symbol language-adapter RPC methods
description: Method registry for the symbol↔language-daemon JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol. Each method's params and result reference types in symbol.rpc.schema.json. Host-agnostic: the same protocol applies whether Python orchestrates Go, Go orchestrates Python, or anything else.

version.description: Protocol major version. Bump only on breaking wire changes.

initialize.description: Handshake. Worker reports its language and version; host advertises its protocol version. Called once per daemon lifetime, before any other method.

language.description: Canonical lowercase language key the worker handles (e.g. 'go').

worker_version.description: Daemon implementation version. Independent of protocol_version.

capabilities.description: Method names this worker supports beyond the v1 minimum (scan_file, validate_syntax). Lets workers ship incrementally — host falls back gracefully when a capability is missing.

scan_file.description: Parse one file and return its full FileScan. Required of every worker.

path.description: Absolute path to the file. Used for diagnostics only; worker reads bytes from `source`.

source.description: UTF-8 file contents. Passed across the wire so the worker doesn't re-read disk.

module_prefix.description: Qualifier prefix the worker should prepend to top-level symbol names. Host computes this from layout (e.g. Python dotted module path, Go package import path).

signature.description: Return the canonical declaration of the first top-level symbol in the provided source, with any function body stripped. Required of every worker. The worker parses with its native AST/printer — never with hand-rolled string state machines.
Empty string if the input has no parseable top-level declaration.

validate_syntax.description: Cheap syntax-only check. Required of every worker.

shutdown.description: Graceful shutdown notification. No response. Worker should flush state and exit.
